title: Halomar Corros
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[:File:JasonFryEmail-20120709.png|unlicensed material is non-canon, per Fry]]}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Corros.jpg]] |name=Halomar Corros |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{JournalCite|1|Chessa's Doom}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him{{ChallengeCite|76|Death by Triflexia}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Fair |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|And that's how I met General Corros and joined the Rebellion.''"
"''No exploding Star Destroyers or big dogfights?|Dirk Harness relates the story of how he met Corros to a group of friends, including an incredulous [[Starter]]|}} '''Halomar Corros''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[General/Legends|general]] who served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Assigned as [[Commanding officer/Legends|commander]] of [[Reginard Base]], Corros oversaw the day-to-day operation of the outpost. Corros was present on the base when a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]], the ''[[Limping Lady]]'', arrived at the outpost carrying independent [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] who were in possession of a [[data cartridge]] containing vital information on [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warship/Legends|warship]] movements. He was later present when the base's senior [[Technician/Legends|technician]], [[Adonar Dellox]], apparently [[Death/Legends|succumbed]] to the deadly disease [[triflexia]]. In reality, he had been murdered by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Tanya Madera]], an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]] who served as the base's second-in-command. Due to Madera's schemes, [[Reginard]] Base was almost discovered by Imperial forces, but Corros's command was saved by a group of Rebel [[Agent/Legends|agents]] stationed at the base. Corros was also responsible for recruiting [[Dirk Harkness]] into the Alliance. He met the young mechanic during a mission to [[Kelada/Legends|Kelada]], where the general hoped to rendezvous with an Alliance [[operative]], [[Chessa Dohhenty]]. Dohhenty was killed by Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] prior to meeting with Corros, and Harkness, who was her lover, killed several stormtroopers in revenge before fleeing the scene. Corros caught up to the young man in a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], [[Lorana's Labyrinth]], and recruited him on the spot. ==Biography== ===Commanding Reginard Base=== {{Quote|Our omnidirectional scanning sensors here in Reginard Base only scan half the planet passively for activity, and only cover half of the Reginard System. The planet blocks us from passively scanning the other hemisphere. We don't use active sensors because those could give our position away.|General Halomar Corros|}} Halomar Corros was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] serving with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Corros held the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[General/Legends|general]] and [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] [[Reginard Base]], a secret Rebel outpost on the uninhabited [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Reginard]], in the [[Pakuuni sector/Legends|Pakuuni sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} {{Unlicensedstart}} Corros had been part of the initial team to establish the outpost on Reginard and had conducted a full mapping survey of the planet. He was responsible for the day-to-day operations of Reginard Base, spending the majority of his [[Time/Legends|time]] in the command center watching monitors and tracking screens. His personal suite at Reginard Base was modest, consisting of only a [[Sleeper (furniture)|bed]], locker, and desk, although he did keep a [[holoportrait]] of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] above his desk. Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],In "[[Limping Lady (article)|Limping Lady]]," a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] in a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] questions patrons about whether or not they had knowledge of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and its crew, indicating the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] set in the [[0 ABY/Legends|year]] leading up to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] had already occurred. Corros welcomed a group of independent [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] aboard the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] ''[[Limping Lady]]'' to Reginard Base. The group had come into possession of a [[data cartridge]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] that contained information on [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. The data cartridge had been in the possession of a [[Unidentified Rebel agent|Rebel agent]] who had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. The spacers had found the coordinates to Reginard Base etched onto the back of the cartridge and sought to deliver the information to the Alliance. Corros was pleased with the spacers' work and expressed interest when they indicated a willingness to join the Alliance. Corros subsequently used the data on the cartridge to log Imperial [[Starship/Legends|starship]] movements.{{ChallengeCite|64|Limping Lady (article)}} ===Crisis on Reginard=== ====The technician's death==== {{Quote|When we first came to Reginard, we mapped the entire planet from orbit. We determined that the best place for the second sensor array would be here, a bare hill rising from the thick forest which covers most of that large continent. You'll have to land a short distance away and transport the array on a repulsor sled. Once you've positioned it and turned off the sled, hook up the medium fusion power generator and turn it on. May the Force be with you.|General Corros briefs a group of Rebel agents on Reginard|}} [[File:Franco.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Captain Franco served under General Carros on Reginard Base.]] At some point during the Galactic Civil War, Reginard Base's power failed. After the emergency back-ups had kicked in, Corros, who had been in the base command center, was called to one of the outpost's storage bays. Along with his second-in-command, [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Tanya Madera]], he arrived to find that a group of Rebel [[Agent/Legends|agents]], stationed at the base as a supply and transport crew, had discovered the body of [[Adonar Dellox]], the base's chief [[Technician/Legends|technician]]. Dellox's face and neck were grotesquely swollen, and he was [[Color/Legends|colored]] a deep red. It appeared that Dellox had become sick from a disease and fallen onto the large piece of equipment on which he was working: a [[passive sensor array]]. The fall damaged the [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] array, which caused a short-circuit and sent a power surge through the cable connecting the array to the rest of the base, which in turn affected the base's power system. Corros was soon joined by the base [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] commander, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tony Franco]]; junior technician [[Kim Yeasson]]; and the base's [[chief medical officer]], [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Akimbo]]. Akimbo immediately set to investigating the incident, ordering a [[detox team]] to secure the corpse and inquiring if any individual had come into contact with the body. As the body was taken away to be [[Autopsy/Legends|autopsied]], Akimbo promised to keep Corros apprised of the situation. The general then set about explaining to the group of Rebel agents that the sensor array on which Dellox had been working was intended to increase Reginard Base's sensor cover in the [[Reginard system/Legends|star system]]. Because the base only used passive omnidirectional sensors to scan the [[Star system/Legends|system]] in order to avoid detection, the Alliance could only scan half the planet and system, since the rest was hidden by Reginard's mass. The passive sensor array was to be installed on an island on the other side of the planet to ensure full sensor coverage. ====The phantom epidemic==== [[File:Akimbo.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Doctor Akimbo conducted extensive testing to diagnose triflexia.]] Several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, Akimbo informed Corros that Dellox had died of [[triflexia]], a contagious and fatal disease that caused [[Blood/Legends|blood]] vessels to swell to three times their normal size. That in turn increased a victim's blood pressure, stopped the [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] exchange in the [[Lung/Legends|lungs]], closed the esophagus, and crushed [[Brain/Legends|brain]] tissue. Corros immediately ordered all base personnel to report to medical for testing, with any personnel that showed possible symptoms being immediately quarantined. Unknown to Corros, the cause for Dellox's death was not as a result of contagion; his second-in-command, Major Madera, was an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]]. She had been assigned to infiltrate an Alliance base, then broadcast its location to allow Imperial forces to attack. To that end, she had installed components into the passive sensor array to broadcast a Stellar Class [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] beacon. Dellox had discovered her handiwork, and she had killed him with a [[Needler/Legends|needler pistol]], which fired darts coated in triflexia [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineered]] by the Empire to work as a [[Poison/Legends|poison]]. While Akimbo worked, he lost another patient to Madera's machinations; she infected an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] who had been quarantined to make it appear as if the triflexia was spreading like a normal disease. In the meantime, the Rebel agents at the base helped to fix the passive sensor array so that it could be deployed, for which Corros was very grateful. ====Imperial inspection==== With the passive sensor array repaired, Corros directed the Rebel agents to use their ship to transport it to the other side of the planet and place it on a bare hill in the middle of a [[Tree/Legends|forest]] on a large island. Once hooked up to a medium [[power fusion generator]], the sensor array would be active. The agents, assisted by Madera, completed the task and headed back to Reginard Base. When they were about an hour away from returning, Corros noted the arrival of a ship with a scrambled [[Transponder code/Legends|transponder]] in-system that was heading straight for the new sensor array. He ordered the agents to turn around and investigate. Upon returning to the sensor array, they discovered the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Hawker Bryce-Kelley]] and his ship, the ''[[Queen's Victory]]''. He was on the run from [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]], had picked up a Stellar Class starport beacon in the system, and had hoped to land at the spaceport. The agents realized that the sensor array had been tampered with, and Madera attacked the group in an effort to stop them from turning off the beacon. The agents were victorious, defeating Madera and shutting down the array. Corros then reported the arrival of an [[Imperial Customs Frigate]] into the system, which was tracking Bryce-Kelley's ship and had briefly detected a starport beacon in an uninhabited system. The agents, in an effort to preserve the secrecy of Reginard Base, intercepted the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Imperial Customs Frigate 517|''517'']] and allowed themselves to be boarded and inspected. The agents acted contrite and helpful toward [[Customs Inspector]] [[Buudro]], and he asked them several questions about their business in the system, and whether or not they had encountered the ''Queen's Victory''. The agents satisfied Buudro with their responses, and he withdrew from the system, leaving Reginard Base undetected. {{Unlicensedend}} ===Kelada=== [[File:Dirk_Harkness.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Dirk Harkness was recruited by Corros into the Alliance.]] {{Quote|Hey, take it easy, son. We're here to help.''"
"''We're friends of Chessa's. We heard something happened to her.|General Corros and Captain Franco meet Dirk Harkness after the death of Chessa Dohhenty|}} Shortly after the destruction of the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] at the Battle of Yavin, Corros and Captain Franco traveled to the planet [[Kelada/Legends|Kelada]] in the [[Duluur sector/Legends|Duluur sector]] of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]. They met with [[Chessa Dohhenty]], an [[operative]] of theirs who was smuggling equipment to the Alliance aboard the ship ''[[Colders Watch]]''. She was discovered in the act by Imperial stormtroopers, who killed her during a [[lightfight]]. Her lover, [[Dirk Harkness]], witnessed the incident and gunned down several stormtroopers before fleeing the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] in which the ''Colders Watch'' was berthed. He eventually ended up in the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] [[Lorana's Labyrinth]]. Corros and Franco learned of Dohennty's fate and tracked Harkness down to Lorana's, where they recruited him into the Alliance as a technician at Reginard Base. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You can tell us about it later. Right now we've got to get you to a safe place.|General Corros consoles Harkness after the death of Chessa Dohhenty|}} Corros was bearded, had thinning [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and was of a fair complexion. {{Unlicensedstart}} A quiet man, he had a tendency to stroke his beard when problems arose. {{Unlicensedend}} ==Skills and abilities== {{Unlicensedstart}} Corros was a capable commander who was knowledgeable about planetary systems and the workings of a [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]]. He was trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. {{Unlicensedend}} ==Equipment== As a general of the Rebel Alliance, Corros wore military attire. {{Unlicensedstart}} The general always carried a blaster along with a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. {{Unlicensedend}} ==Behind the scenes== Halomar Corros first appeared in "[[Limping Lady (article)|Limping Lady]]," an article written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]] and published in the [[Challenge 64|sixth-fourth issue]] of the [[Challenge (magazine)|''Challenge'']] magazine in [[September]] [[1992]]. His first name was introduced in the article "[[Death by Triflexia]]", written by Schweighofer and published in the [[Challenge 64|seventy-sixth issue]] of the ''Challenge'' magazine in [[1995]]. Corros's appearances in the articles published in ''Challenge'' are ambiguously [[canon]] within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity as they were released outside of the West End Games license. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|1|Chessa's Doom}} {{Flash}} ===Non-canon appearances=== *{{ChallengeCite|64|Limping Lady (article)}} {{1st}} {{Un}} *{{ChallengeCite|76|Death by Triflexia}} {{1stID|Halomar}} {{Un}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Halomar Corros }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Corros, Halomar}} [[Category:Alliance admirals and generals]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
